steps: pET17b-Kif5b(1-560)-GFP-His should be transformed into BL21(DE3)RIPL cells.
Make enough LB for at least 2 L of culture.
Transfer starter culture into LB. Make sure to add proper antibiotics Allow it to grow at 200 rpm, 37 °C, until OD600 reaches 0.6-0.8.
Add 0.75 millimolar (mM), IPTG and allow it to grow at 200 rpm, 18 °C, 16 hours
Harvest pellets by centrifugation, wash in ice cold PBS and freeze in liquid nitrogen until use.

Resuspend 2 L worth of pellets in 40 mL, Lysis buffer supplemented with 1 cOmplete EDTA-free protease inhibitor cocktail tablet (Roche) per 40 mL, of Lysis buffer . Also add 1 mL of 50 mg/mL, lysozyme . Vortex vigorously to resuspend. Incubate On ice for 00:30:00 .
Lyse the resuspension by sonication on ice with pulse cycles 00:00:05 on and 00:00:20 rest.
Clarify by centrifugation 92600 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes in Type 70 Ti rotor (Beckman).
Incubate the supernatant with Ni-NTA agarose beads washed with the Wash buffer . Nutate for 01:00:00 at 4 °C in the dark (from this step onwards, keep in dark as much as possible).
Apply to gravity column and wash 4 times with 10 mL, Wash buffer each time.
Resuspend the beads in 1 mL elution buffer, incubate for 00:02:00 and elute in 0.5 mL increments. Repeat elution step up to 5 times (following green GFP signal)
Combine peak fractions and buffer exchange on PD-10 desalting column equilibrated with Storage buffer (BRB80) .
Peak fractions of the motor solution were flash-frozen at -80 °C until further use. Bind and release protocol could be used at this stage, see: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bp2l61xrdvqe/v1
During centrifugation, wash each 4 mL Ni-NTA agarose slurry with 20 mL Wash buffer .
creator: Katherine Surridge
